description: Collects provider-specific configuration not covered by Axon credentialing: clinical philosophy, voice directives, autonomy tiers, and consent.
llm_system_prompt: You are CareAgent, a clinical AI assistant helping a healthcare provider configure their agent. Be professional, warm, and concise. Guide them through setting their clinical philosophy, voice preferences, autonomy tiers, and consent acknowledgment.
completion_criteria: All required fields collected: philosophy, autonomy tiers for all 7 actions, and consent acknowledgment.

sections.title: Clinical Philosophy
sections.description: The provider's approach to clinical AI assistance.

sections.title: Voice Directives
sections.description: Optional per-action instructions for how CareAgent should behave.

sections.title: Autonomy Configuration
sections.description: Per-action autonomy tiers controlling CareAgent's independence.

sections.title: Consent Acknowledgment
sections.description: HIPAA, synthetic data, and audit consent verification.

questions.text: Describe your clinical philosophy — how should your AI assistant approach clinical work?
questions.llm_guidance: Ask the provider to describe in their own words how they want their AI assistant to approach clinical decision-making. Encourage them to mention priorities like evidence-based practice, patient autonomy, conservative vs. aggressive approaches, etc.

questions.text: Do you have specific instructions for how CareAgent should handle clinical documentation (charting)?
questions.llm_guidance: This is optional. Examples: 'Use structured SOAP format', 'Always include assessment and plan', 'Keep notes concise'. If the provider says no or skips, that's fine.

questions.text: Do you have specific instructions for how CareAgent should handle patient education?
questions.llm_guidance: Optional. Examples: 'Include visual diagrams', 'Write at 6th-grade reading level', 'Always mention lifestyle modifications'.

questions.text: Do you have specific instructions for how CareAgent should interpret clinical results?
questions.llm_guidance: Optional. Examples: 'Always compare to previous results', 'Flag critical values prominently', 'Include reference ranges'.

questions.text: What autonomy level for clinical documentation (charting)?

options.label: Autonomous
options.description: CareAgent acts independently, you review afterward

options.label: Supervised
options.description: CareAgent prepares the work, you approve before execution

options.label: Manual
options.description: You must explicitly initiate, CareAgent only assists when asked

questions.llm_guidance: Most providers choose 'autonomous' for charting. Explain the three tiers clearly.

questions.text: What autonomy level for orders (medications, labs, imaging)?
questions.llm_guidance: Most providers choose 'supervised' or 'manual' for orders due to safety implications.

questions.text: What autonomy level for billing and coding?
What autonomy level for clinical procedures?
What autonomy level for result interpretation?
What autonomy level for patient education?
questions.llm_guidance: Most providers choose 'autonomous' for patient education.

questions.text: What autonomy level for care coordination (referrals, transitions)?
Do you confirm: (1) HIPAA warning acknowledged, (2) synthetic data only, (3) consent to audit logging?
questions.llm_guidance: This is a final consent confirmation. The provider must agree to all three points. If they decline, the onboarding cannot proceed.
